item.title: ‘We Palestinians Are Not Going to Give Up’
item.description: The Palestinian advocate Rula Hardal is an executive director of A Land for All, an Israeli-Palestinian initiative to imagine a new future for the region. She shares her motivation to find new solutions on “The Ezra Klein Show.”

item.title: Why Israelis and Palestinians Need New Narratives
item.description: A Land for All is an Israeli-Palestinian initiative advocating options beyond the two-state or one-state solution — namely, a confederacy model in which Israel and Palestine would exist as separate political entities but with free movement across borders. On “The Ezra Klein Show,” the initiative’s executive directors, May Pundak and Rula Hardal, explain their long-term vision for the region and why both Israelis and Palestinians need new national narratives.

item.title: The American Economy Isn’t as Bad as You Might Think
item.description: Influencers may be Chinamaxxing, but the U.S. is still the world’s economic powerhouse.

item.title: The American Economy Isn’t as Bad as You Might Think
item.description: Influencers may be Chinamaxxing, but the U.S. is still the world’s economic powerhouse.

item.title: Is This Liberalism’s Last Stand?
item.description: The “death certificates” issued for liberalism, one analyst says, are “premature.”

item.title: ‘It’s Time for Celebrities to Step Up’
item.description: If activists are speaking up, why aren’t celebrities? According to Molly Jong-Fast, there’s value to performative politics. Especially when they dominate the algorithm.

item.title: How GLP-1s Feed Our Sick Culture
item.description: For women, they’re a depressingly rational choice.

item.title: This Is One of the Hardest Jobs in America. Millions Are Doing It Alone.
item.description: Behind closed doors, millions of Americans are stepping into one of the hardest roles they’ll ever take on: caring for their aging parents.

item.title: ‘You Do It Because They’re Your Parents’
item.description: Behind closed doors, millions of Americans are stepping into one of the hardest roles they’ll ever take on: caring for their aging parents.

item.title: ‘This Has Not Given Us Safety’
item.description: The Israeli activist May Pundak advocated the two-state solution for many years, but her confidence faded as she saw the idea lose relevance. On “The Ezra Klein Show,” Pundak explains why there’s a need for a new way of thinking — and a new plan for the future of the region.

item.title: ‘Trump’s Intervention Had an Impact’: 3 Writers on America’s World Cup Exit
item.description: The world’s most global game produces some of its strongest feelings of national belonging.

item.title: We Are Losing the Ability to Discover What We Didn’t Know to Ask
item.description: What used to be a meandering journey is now an immediate arrival at your destination.

item.title: Democratic Socialists Are on the Rise. We’ve Seen This Movie Before.
item.description: Mainstream party leaders can’t dodge a fight with their left.

item.title: Whose Land? Our Land, Not Trump’s.
item.description: On a day celebrating harmony, the president sang a tune of “This land belongs to me and mine.”

item.title: Trump and the U.S. Loss in the World Cup
item.description: Readers react to Belgium’s victory and President Trump’s meddling. Also: Effects of Supreme Court rulings; hanging out without phones.

item.title: JD Vance Is Worried
item.description: The vice president’s theology comes in book form.

item.title: Stop Stressing About Sleep
item.description: The evidence for needing eight hours of sleep a night is shakier than we might assume.

item.title: America Was Being Played. The Bessent Doctrine Says Those Days Are Over.
item.description: The United States is changing the rules of trade in its favor.

item.title: Lessons From the Graham Platner Disaster
item.description: Many Democrats want representatives as furious as they are. That makes it easier for unfit candidates to escape proper vetting.

item.title: A Radical Vision for Israelis and Palestinians
item.description: The old solutions to the Israeli-Palestinian conflict don’t seem to fit the present reality. A Land for All proposes an alternative.

item.title: A Radical Vision for Israelis and Palestinians
item.description: The old solutions to the Israeli-Palestinian conflict don’t seem to fit the present reality. A Land for All proposes an alternative.

item.title: Behind the Scenes of NATO, Europe Is Falling Apart
item.description: The greatest dangers lie within.

item.title: Why A.I. Won’t Give Us a Four-Day Workweek
item.description: The very leaders rhapsodizing about a shorter workweek are demanding more time, not less, from their employees.

item.title: It Failed in France. It Would Be a Disaster in California.
item.description: California might bet its future on the notion that billionaires won’t leave, but they are already fleeing the state.

item.title: Bring Back the SAT, California
item.description: Under the test-blind policy, more students are thrown into classes for which they are unprepared.

item.title: Trump Wins the FIFA Appease Prize
item.description: Is it fair that players live in one reality and referees live in another? Is it fair that President Trump trumps them all?

item.title: Trump’s International U.F.C. Diplomacy
item.description: For Trump, cage fighting is the new “Kumbaya.”

item.title: Trump Cashes In While Others Lose Out
item.description: Readers react to President Trump’s windfall. Also: Mr. Trump’s intervention in the World Cup; Taylor Swift, feminist bride; rich political donors.

item.title: If Europe Wants to Save NATO, It’s Doing All the Wrong Things
item.description: It’s time for the rest of the alliance to get its act together.

item.title: The New, Unsustainable Art World
item.description: Readers respond to a guest essay by a former art fair director about the struggles that galleries face.

item.title: The Bureaucratic Hell of Getting a Job in 2026
Israel Is Not Invincible
item.description: There is suddenly daylight between the Trump administration and Netanyahu.

item.title: Israel Is Not Invincible
item.description: There is suddenly daylight between the Trump administration and Netanyahu.

item.title: Five Questions That Will Determine America’s Next 50 Years
item.description: The truth worth celebrating this summer is that our country is still being made.

item.title: Five Questions That Will Determine America’s Next 50 Years
item.description: The truth worth celebrating this summer is that our country is still being made.

item.title: What We Should Learn From Nordic Happiness
item.description: Scandinavia is the land of five-week vacations, virtually free university, one-year parental leaves, easy child care and very happy citizens.

item.title: What We Should Learn From Nordic Happiness
item.description: Scandinavia is the land of five-week vacations, virtually free university, one-year parental leaves, easy child care and very happy citizens.

item.title: The Liberal American Jew’s Dilemma
item.description: Over the years, I have moved toward the way of being Jewish that my father rejected.

item.title: Dating Your Co-Workers Is Fun. It’s Sexy. Go Ahead, Do It.
item.description: Meeting a partner at work used to be common. Now? Not so much. But office romance can be sexy — plus, it’s got a better success rate than the apps.

item.title: Mexico’s World Cup Moment Is Pure, Fragile Magic
item.description: In Mexico City, World Cup euphoria is overshadowing political gloom.

item.title: What Does It Mean to Love America?
item.description: America celebrates its 250th birthday this year. The Opinion columnist Ezra Klein asks the civil rights lawyer Bryan Stevenson what he thinks American greatness is really about.

item.title: Obama vs. Trump Is the Election Day We Needed but Never Got
item.description: The proxy battles we got instead have not settled questions we have to settle.

item.title: I’m a Therapist. Not Everyone Should Be in Therapy.
item.description: Many patients misunderstand what therapy reliably provides.

item.title: The Art of Summitry
item.description: The performance is meant to imply that the world is an orderly place, or at least a place on which reasonable people are determined to impose some order.

item.title: Technology Ruined Our Lazy Days at the Lake
item.description: Living at the lake house taught me about risk taking and other life lessons.

item.title: We Hate to Break It to You, but Maybe We’re Not That Polarized
item.description: Americans have been told for at least two decades that they share less and less, while the data show the actual overlap holding roughly steady.

item.title: The Unifying Speech We Won’t Get This Independence Day
item.description: America, we need a new beginning.

item.title: The Unifying Speech We Won’t Get This Independence Day
item.description: America, we need a new beginning.

item.title: Founding Father vs. Foundering Toddler
item.description: No. 1 puts No. 45/47 to shame.

item.title: Founding Father vs. Foundering Toddler
item.description: No. 1 puts No. 45/47 to shame.

item.title: The Unifying Speech We Won’t Get This Independence Day
item.description: America, we need a new beginning.

item.title: Mary Beard Looks at Trump and Sees Roman Emperors
item.description: Arches, gladiatorial fights, portraits on money, flattery. What century is this?

item.title: How to Get School Policy Back on Track
item.description: Readers respond to “‘No Child Left Behind’ Nostalgia Is Delusional,” an Opinion guest essay by Ross Wiener.

item.title: This Pathetic Groveling Is No Way to Rebuild a Party
item.description: The Democrats’ constant solicitations for cash do not make their supporters feel like they are on a winning team.

item.title: How to World Cup
item.description: New to soccer? You’re welcome here.

item.title: A.I. Is Strangling Our Economy
item.description: So much money is being poured into A.I. that the rest of the economy is starting to suffer.

item.title: The Transgender Sports Decision Was About Something Deeper Than Law
item.description: It’s never just a game.

item.title: Five Words That Shook the World
item.description: It wasn’t Thomas Jefferson who made “All men are created equal” actually mean something.

item.title: Parents Can’t Give Their Kids Everything. Siblings Can Help.
item.description: There are virtues in growing up in a pack.

item.title: The Trump Vibe Shift Was Just a Mirage
item.description: The country does not love its president.

item.title: Elon Musk Is Too Rich. We’ve Known That Since Plato.
item.description: The philosopher made a radical proposal about wealth.

item.title: Creature From the Green Lagoon
item.description: Trump’s slimy, stinky swamp within the swamp.

item.title: My Father’s Death Was the Start of My Life
item.description: It was the worst thing that ever happened to me. It also taught me everything I needed to live.

item.title: Trump Was Right. The War Ended With a Surrender.
item.description: Each generation seems destined to learn anew the lesson that when hawks declare that a war will be painless, beware.

item.title: We Need to Keep Funding Weird Science
item.description: Science turned a serendipitous finding about lizard venom into one of the most important drugs of the century, but that type of research is getting harder to do.

item.title: In Britain, the Far Right Is Moving to the Mainstream
item.description: Its opponents urgently need to demonstrate that they can do more than throw up their hands in horror.

item.title: We Ran the Numbers. Remote Work Is Bad for Us.
item.description: Despite its advantages, work-from-home arrangements have significantly deepened Americans’ isolation and distress.

item.title: Why the Knicks Really Won
item.description: Teamwork isn’t just useful, the Knicks showed. It’s a higher calling.

item.title: JD Vance on the Morality of the Trump Administration
item.description: I asked the vice president what is Christian about this White House.
